paper_focus.title: Enabling superior impact toughness of low-density δ-ferrite steel by dispersing ultra-fine spheroidized carbides in the ferrite/carbide composite lamellae
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate the synergistic effect of rolling temperature on microstructure and fracture toughness in low-density δ-ferrite steel and to reveal the mechanisms by which ultra-fine spherical carbides in lamellar structures enhance impact resistance.
material_systems: Phases: δ-ferrite, martensite, ultra-fine spherical carbides (cementite), deformation-induced ferrite (DIF)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (lamellar morphology, carbide size distribution, phase fractions, grain structures)
property (impact toughness, tensile strength, elongation, hardness)
mechanism (Orowan strengthening, crack tip blunting, crack deflection, microvoid nucleation and horizontal crack propagation)
figure_evidence (TEM, HRTEM, EBSD, SEM fractography, schematics)
